steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is a compound: 'a state's voting record and polling data' — two items joined by 'and,' so the subject is plural. The parenthetical 'not its population size' does not change that. The blank needs a plural finite verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'determines': singular (-s ending). A compound subject cannot take a singular verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'has determined': singular auxiliary 'has.' Same number error. ✗
  • Choice C — 'determining': a participle, not a finite verb. Leaves the sentence without a main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — 'determine': plural base form. 'Voting record and polling data determine its importance' — subject and verb agree. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
